episodes.title: 1 in 5 Recent Terrorism Arrests Are Children | Online Safety for Parents
episodes.description:
One in five terrorism arrests in the UK in recent years has involved a child under 18.

Detective Superintendent Jane Corrigan from Counter Terrorism Policing explains how children are being drawn into terrorism, radicalisation, and accessing extreme online content.

Det Supt Corrigan reflects on why the TV drama Adolescence resonated so strongly with parents and aspects of the story that appear in counter terrorism case work. She explores the risks of online content, the way young people are being groomed, as well as offering practical advice on how parents can support their child.

Get advice and support: ActEarly.uk

Chapters
00:00 - 1 in 5 Recent Terrorism Arrests are Children
00:21 - Why Adolescence Sparked Debate with Parents
01:28 - A Shift to Young People Being Drawn into Terrorism
02:03 - Behaviours
02:22 - Understanding Online Risk for Children
02:40 - Advice and Support for Parents
